findings.impact: The dashboard is nearly empty — four plain cards with single-letter icons on a vast dark background. No recent activity, no canvas previews, no quick actions. Users land here with zero context about what to do next.
findings.current_state: Four flat cards (Canvas, Databases, Marketplace, Settings) with muted teal letter-icons, minimal descriptions, no imagery or data.
findings.recommendation: Transform into a rich home screen: recent canvases with live thumbnails, activity feed, quick-create actions, usage stats widget, and getting-started prompts for new users.
findings.design_direction: glassmorphism cards, ambient glow, hero section with canvas previews, spatial depth layers

findings.impact: The top nav is functional but flat — plain text links with no visual weight differentiation. Right-side icon cluster (profile, messages, notifications, settings, dev) is hard to parse at small sizes with no labels or tooltips visible.
findings.current_state: Flat dark nav bar with text links left-aligned, icon buttons right-aligned. Active state is a subtle white box around the link text. No hover states or animations visible.
findings.recommendation: Add subtle glass-panel treatment to the nav. Use icon+label pairs or tooltip on hover for right-side actions. Add a gradient underline or glow effect for active state instead of a box.
findings.design_direction: frosted glass nav bar, subtle gradient active indicators, micro-animation on hover, icon tooltips

findings.impact: The canvas toolbar is a floating pill in the center-top with minimal controls (canvas name, undo/redo, zoom, preview toggle, fullscreen). No tool selection (pen, select, shape, text) is visible. Users have no clear way to add content to the canvas.
findings.current_state: Dark pill-shaped toolbar with 'Untitled Canvas' text, green dot (unsaved?), undo/redo arrows, zoom controls (- 80% +), Preview button, and fullscreen icon.
findings.recommendation: Add a visible tool palette (select, pen, shape, text, sticker) either as a left sidebar or as an expandable section of the toolbar. Make the canvas name editable with a visible edit affordance. Add a more prominent 'Add Widget' button.
findings.design_direction: floating glass toolbar with tool icons, radial tool menu, spatial depth, glow-on-hover tool icons

findings.impact: The Canvas Docker panel on the right is a plain bordered rectangle with 'Widgets' tab and a '+' button. The clock widget inside is barely visible (faint text). The panel feels like a V4 leftover — no glass effect, no depth, no visual polish.
findings.current_state: Thin-bordered dark panel with 'Canvas Docker' title, pin/fullscreen/minimize/close icons, 'Widgets' tab, and a faintly visible clock widget showing time.
findings.recommendation: Redesign as a modern floating panel with glassmorphism, rounded corners, subtle shadow/glow. Widget cards inside should have thumbnails and clear labels. The Docker concept should be more visually explained.
findings.design_direction: glassmorphism panel, floating with shadow, widget thumbnails as cards, ambient border glow

findings.impact: The 'Assets' tab on the left edge and 'Props' tab on the right edge are extremely small vertical text labels that are easy to miss. They appear to be toggles for side panels but their affordance is nearly invisible.
findings.current_state: Tiny vertical text ('Assets' on left, 'Props' on right) rotated 90 degrees at the very edge of the viewport, using a very small font size with no background or icon.
findings.recommendation: Replace with icon-based tab handles that expand on hover, showing the label. Use a subtle glass panel background behind the tab handle. Add a slide-in animation for the panel they control.
findings.design_direction: icon tabs with expand-on-hover labels, slide-in glass panels, animated transitions

findings.impact: A brand new canvas shows absolutely nothing — no onboarding prompt, no 'drag to add' hint, no grid, no center marker. Users are dropped into a dark void with no guidance on how to start creating.
findings.current_state: Completely blank dark canvas area with subtle gradient background. No grid lines, no welcome message, no placeholder content.
findings.recommendation: Add a centered onboarding overlay for new canvases: 'Right-click to add widgets, drag from Assets panel, or use keyboard shortcuts.' Show a subtle dot grid. Optionally offer starter templates.
findings.design_direction: subtle dot grid, centered ghost prompt with glass card, animated intro sequence, template picker overlay

findings.impact: Database card icons are rendering as raw text ('users', 'bug', 'calendar') that overflows the icon area, breaking the card layout. The letter-in-square icon (e.g., 'D' for Fist Document) is inconsistent with the text-overflow icons. Duplicate 'CRM Contacts' entries suggest a data bug.
findings.current_state: Cards with mixed icon treatments: some show a teal square with a letter (D), others show spilling colored text ('users', 'bug', 'calendar'). Two identical 'CRM Contacts' cards appear. Card has edit (pen) and delete (x) icons, type/scope badges, description text, and date.
findings.recommendation: Standardize all database card icons to use a consistent icon system (e.g., Lucide icons for doc, table, note types). Fix the icon overflow. Deduplicate the CRM entries. Add card hover states with shadow elevation.
findings.design_direction: consistent icon system, elevated card hover, glass card treatment, proper icon containers

findings.impact: The header row has 'Databases' title on the left and three action buttons (+ New Database, Import from Notion, Use Template) on the right, but the buttons feel crowded together and their visual weight is inconsistent — the primary action isn't clearly differentiated.
findings.current_state: '+ New Database' has a teal filled button, 'Import from Notion' and 'Use Template' have outlined/ghost buttons. All three are similar size and proximity.
findings.recommendation: Make '+ New Database' the clear primary CTA with larger size or accent glow. Group 'Import from Notion' and 'Use Template' as secondary actions with a dropdown or smaller styling. Add more spacing between the groups.
findings.design_direction: primary CTA with glow effect, secondary action dropdown, clear visual hierarchy

findings.impact: The Lab's pipeline canvas shows two empty dashed-outline nodes with a connecting line and the text 'Add entities to start building your pipeline.' The empty state is functional but visually bland and doesn't guide the user through first steps.
findings.current_state: Two dashed rectangles connected by a dashed line in the center. Small text prompt below. A floating white element in bottom-left corner appears to be a minimap or palette that's partially cut off.
findings.recommendation: Add an interactive onboarding: 'Click an entity type from the left panel to place your first node.' Use animated arrows or highlighted paths. The empty node placeholders should pulse or glow to invite interaction.
findings.design_direction: animated onboarding arrows, pulsing placeholder nodes, guided first-interaction flow

findings.impact: The left sidebar entity list (Content, Visual, Spatial, Structure categories) uses a plain tree-view with small icons and text. Categories are capitalized labels with disclosure triangles. The hierarchy is readable but feels utilitarian rather than polished.
findings.current_state: Flat sidebar with category headers (CONTENT, VISUAL, SPATIAL, STRUCTURE), each with sub-items (Widget, Sticker, Text, Audio, Lottie, etc.). Small icons precede each item name. 'Add to Canvas' button at bottom.
findings.recommendation: Add category icons or colored section dividers. Use card-style grouping with subtle backgrounds per category. Add drag handles or hover previews for entity types. Make the sidebar feel like a creative palette, not a file tree.
findings.design_direction: creative palette with colored category sections, entity type previews on hover, glass-panel sidebar

findings.impact: The AI generation bar at the bottom ('Describe a widget... Generate') is compact but under-emphasized. The model selector ('Kimi K2.5') is a small pill tag. This is a major feature that should feel more prominent and inviting.
findings.current_state: Bottom-center bar with gray dot, 'Kimi K2.5' tag, text input 'Describe a widget...', and 'Generate' button. Minimal visual treatment.
findings.recommendation: Make the AI generation bar a hero feature: larger input field with a gradient border or glow effect, animated placeholder text showing example prompts, prominent model selector with dropdown. Consider making it expandable into a full generation panel.
findings.design_direction: gradient-border input, animated placeholder examples, expandable AI panel, prominent generate CTA with glow

findings.impact: The bottom status bar shows useful dev info (Connected, Paused, Branch: main, Latency: 12ms) but it's very small and could be more useful with additional context like widget count or save status.
findings.current_state: Thin bottom bar with green dot 'Connected', 'Paused', 'Branch: main', 'Latency: 12ms', and 'Untitled Widget' on the right.
findings.recommendation: Keep the compact format but add save status indicator, entity count, and a clickable connection indicator that shows detailed sync info on click.
findings.design_direction: compact info bar with interactive elements, connection pulse animation

findings.impact: Widget cards use large gradient thumbnails with a single letter (D, C, P, M) as placeholder. This works for seed data but real widgets need actual preview images. The card layout is solid but the placeholder thumbnails make the marketplace feel empty/fake.
findings.current_state: Cards with gradient-colored header showing a large letter + small icon + category badge, followed by name, description, rating stars, install count, price, and Install/Buy button.
findings.recommendation: For seed data, generate more visually interesting placeholder thumbnails (mini-screenshots or abstract widget previews). Ensure the publish pipeline captures real thumbnails. Add hover animation to cards (subtle lift + shadow).
findings.design_direction: card hover elevation, richer placeholder thumbnails, glass overlay on hover showing quick-actions

findings.impact: The featured carousel has left/right arrow navigation and dot indicators, but the arrows overlap the first/last card content (the left arrow covers part of the Dice Roller card). Dot indicators are very small.
findings.current_state: Horizontal card carousel with '<' and '>' circular buttons that overlap card edges. Small dot pagination indicators below.
findings.recommendation: Move carousel arrows outside the card area or use semi-transparent overlays that don't block content. Make dot indicators slightly larger with active state glow.
findings.design_direction: non-overlapping carousel controls, larger pagination dots with glow

findings.impact: The profile page looks like a social media profile (banner, avatar, bio, stats) which is appropriate for the social features, but the banner is a generic purple gradient and the avatar is a plain letter circle. The stats section (Followers, Following, Posts, Canvases) feels disconnected from the StickerNest identity.
findings.current_state: Purple gradient banner, teal circle avatar with 'K', name/username, bio, join date, and four stat counters. 'Edit Profile' button in the top-right.
findings.recommendation: Allow custom banners and avatars (already may be supported). Add featured canvases section. Make the stats more visual (mini-charts or animated counters). Add canvas showcase grid below the stats.
findings.design_direction: featured canvas showcase, animated stat counters, customizable banner with canvas preview

findings.impact: Clicking 'Canvas' in the top navigation redirects to /profile/me instead of showing a canvas list or the last-used canvas. This is confusing — users expect 'Canvas' to go to the canvas workspace, not their profile.
findings.current_state: The /canvas route redirects to /profile/me. The canvas list is buried at the bottom of the profile page.
findings.recommendation: Fix routing: /canvas should show a canvas list/picker page (recent canvases, shared canvases, new canvas CTA). The profile page's canvas section can link to individual canvases but shouldn't be the primary entry point.
findings.design_direction: dedicated canvas list page with thumbnails, recent-first sorting, prominent new canvas CTA

findings.impact: The Settings page is sparse — Billing tab shows plan info and usage bars, but a lot of vertical space is wasted. The 'Sign Out' button feels orphaned at the bottom with a horizontal divider above it. Other tabs (Integrations, Creator Commerce, My Purchases, Security) are not shown but the layout pattern suggests they may be similarly sparse.
findings.current_state: Centered content column with tab navigation. Billing section has two glass-panel cards (Pro Plan + Usage). Large empty space. Red 'Sign Out' button at the bottom.
findings.recommendation: Use a left-sidebar tab navigation instead of top tabs to fill horizontal space. Group related settings visually. Move Sign Out to a less prominent location (e.g., in a user menu dropdown). Fill the usage section with more detail (storage breakdown, canvas list).
findings.design_direction: left-sidebar settings navigation, richer usage visualizations, compact sign-out placement

findings.impact: The messages page shows 'Unknown User' for both conversations, indicating a data resolution issue. The left panel conversation list is functional but visually plain. The empty right panel says 'Select a conversation to start messaging' — adequate but uninspired.
findings.current_state: Left panel: narrow list with teal 'U' avatars, 'Unknown User' labels, message previews, and relative timestamps (2h, 1d). Right panel: centered empty state text.
findings.recommendation: Fix user resolution to show actual names/avatars. Add a 'New Message' button. Style conversation list items with hover states, unread indicators, and online status dots. Make the empty state more inviting with an illustration.
findings.design_direction: rich conversation list with status indicators, illustrated empty state, new message CTA

findings.impact: The app uses a dark gradient background with subtle teal/purple tones that shifts slightly per page. While atmospheric, it feels flat and samey across all screens. There's no visual texture or depth beyond the gradient.
findings.current_state: Dark (#1a1a2e-ish) background with subtle radial gradients in teal and purple. Consistent across all pages. No noise, no grid, no particles, no depth layers.
findings.recommendation: Add subtle animated noise texture or mesh gradient that shifts slowly. Use layered depth (background → mid-ground blur → foreground content) to create a spatial feel consistent with the 'spatial operating system' brand.
findings.design_direction: animated mesh gradient background, noise texture overlay, layered depth with parallax, spatial OS atmosphere

findings.impact: Typography is functional but lacks personality. Headings, body text, and labels all feel like defaults. No display font or distinctive type treatment that says 'this is StickerNest.'
findings.current_state: Sans-serif font (appears to be Inter or system font) used consistently. Headings are bold, body is regular. No special display treatments.
findings.recommendation: Consider a distinctive heading font (geometric sans like Space Grotesk or Satoshi) for page titles and section headers while keeping body text readable. Add subtle letter-spacing adjustments for labels.
findings.design_direction: geometric heading font, refined letter-spacing, type hierarchy with personality

findings.impact: Sample widget cards use a different layout from Marketplace browse cards — they have difficulty badges (Beginner, Intermediate, Advanced), feature tags (state, events, theme, config), and Download/Try Upload buttons. The style is consistent within this page but differs from the Browse cards.
findings.current_state: Glass-panel cards with title, difficulty badge, description, feature tags as pills, and two action buttons (Download, Try Upload).
findings.recommendation: This is actually a well-designed card pattern. Consider bringing some elements back to the main Marketplace cards (feature tags, difficulty/complexity indicators). Align the visual treatment between both card types.
findings.design_direction: unified card system across Marketplace tabs, shared tag/badge components
